panels.description: Shows whether Prometheus can scrape the service. Value 1 means the service is reachable, value 0 means it is down or metrics endpoint is unavailable.
panels.title: Service Up

panels.description: Shows CPU usage of the Java process.
panels.title: CPU Usage

panels.description: Shows overall system CPU usage on the machine where the service is running.
panels.title: System CPU Usage

panels.description: Shows the current number of live JVM threads. A constant increase can indicate thread leaks, blocked workers, or unbounded async/task execution.
panels.title: Live Threads

panels.description: Total number of successful HTTP responses during the selected time range. Internal Prometheus scrape requests are excluded.
panels.title: 2xx Responses

panels.description: Total number of 4xx HTTP responses during the selected time range.
panels.title: 4xx Responses

panels.description: Total number of 5xx HTTP responses during the selected time range.
panels.title: 5xx Responses

panels.description: Average number of successful 2xx HTTP responses per second during the selected time range.
panels.title: Average 2xx RPS

panels.description: Average number of 4xx HTTP responses per second during the selected time range.
panels.title: Average 4xx RPS

panels.description: Average number of 5xx HTTP responses per second during the selected time range.
panels.title: Average 5xx RPS

panels.description: Average HTTP request processing time grouped by endpoint and method for the selected service. Internal Prometheus scrape requests are excluded.

targets.expr: sum by(uri, method) (rate(http_server_requests_seconds_sum{service=~"$service_name", environment=~"$env", uri!="UNKNOWN", uri!="/actuator/prometheus", uri!="/**"}[$__rate_interval])) / (sum by(uri, method) (rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{service=~"$service_name", environment=~"$env", uri!="UNKNOWN", uri!="/actuator/prometheus", uri!="/**"}[$__rate_interval])))

panels.title: Average Latency by Endpoint

panels.description: Shows how many database connections are currently in use by the application. High active connections for a long time may indicate slow queries or insufficient pool size.
panels.title: Active Connections

panels.description: Shows how many database connections are open but currently unused. This helps understand whether the connection pool has spare capacity.
panels.title: Idle Connections

panels.description: Shows how many threads are waiting for a database connection from the pool. Any sustained value above zero usually means the pool is exhausted or queries are too slow.
panels.title: Pending Connections

panels.description: Shows the average time a database connection stays checked out by application code. High values can indicate slow transactions, long queries, or connection leaks.

targets.expr:
sum by (service, pool) (
  rate(hikaricp_connections_usage_seconds_sum{service=~"$service_name", environment=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval])
)
/
sum by (service, pool) (
  rate(hikaricp_connections_usage_seconds_count{service=~"$service_name", environment=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval])
)

panels.title: Connection Usage Avg

panels.description: Shows the average time needed to obtain a database connection from the pool. Higher values mean requests are waiting for available DB connections.

targets.expr:
sum by (service, pool) (
  rate(hikaricp_connections_acquire_seconds_sum{service=~"$service_name", environment=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval])
)
/
sum by (service, pool) (
  rate(hikaricp_connections_acquire_seconds_count{service=~"$service_name", environment=~"$env"}[$__rate_interval])
)

panels.title: Connection Acquire Avg
